name: Biscotti - the Dipping Cookie
recipeInstructions:
The "Bis" means twice and "Cotti" means cooked - our recipe for these crisp, crunchy cookies does just that. They are best when dipped in hot coffee, cocoa, or milk for a few seconds until softened, then eaten right away.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Toast nuts until browned, about 8 minutes; let cool.
Using a metal blade in the food processor work bowl, finely chop zest and granulated sugar for about 60 seconds. Add brown sugar, extracts, margarine, and eggs; process until combined, about 5 seconds. Add nuts and dry ingredients; pulse until combined, about 12 times.
Transfer dough to a floured surface; roll into two 12-inch logs. Pat logs to 2 1/2 inches wide. Place logs 3 inches apart on lightly greased baking sheets.
Beat egg white slightly and brush over the surface of the logs. Bake for 20 minutes until firm and lightly browned. Remove from oven; reduce heat to 325°F. While still warm, cut logs diagonally into 1/2-inch slices, lay cut side down on sheets. Bake for an additional 15 minutes until dry. Cool on wire racks.
Store in a plastic food storage bag or tin for up to 1 month.
Note: Nutritional analysis: Calories 46, Saturated fat 0g, total fat 1g, carbohydrate 7g, protein 1g. Cholesterol 11mg. Sodium 17 mg.
